question_text: Which documentation entry best reflects factual RN case management charting?
answer_a: Patient stated the pharmacy delivery did not arrive; RN contacted pharmacy and planned follow-up after delivery confirmation.
answer_b: Patient is careless with medications and needs better discipline.
answer_c: Patient probably misunderstood the discharge plan, so RN will wait until next week.
rationale: Factual charting identifies the source, barrier, RN action, and follow-up without using judgmental labels or unsupported conclusions.

question_text: What is the safest approach when an RN case manager must document a call after the actual contact occurred?
answer_a: Backdate the note so the record looks complete in chronological order.
answer_b: Enter a late entry according to policy, identify the event date or time when known, and document only accurate facts.
answer_c: Wait until every related referral is closed before documenting the original call.
answer_d: Ask another team member to enter the note if they have more time.
rationale: A late entry can be appropriate when it is labeled and factual; backdating or delaying until all follow-up is closed weakens the record.

question_text: A patient reports new shortness of breath and a rapid weight increase after discharge. Which note best documents RN case management provider notification?
answer_b: Told patient to discuss symptoms at the next visit if still present.
answer_c: At 1015, RN contacted cardiology triage, reported patient-stated shortness of breath and weight increase, requested same-day guidance, and documented callback plan.
answer_d: Provider notified, no further documentation needed.
rationale: The best note includes time, recipient, clinical concern, information shared, requested response, and follow-up plan while staying within RN scope.

question_text: An RN case manager notices that yesterday's note listed the wrong referral agency. What action best preserves the integrity of the record?
answer_a: Delete the old note and enter a corrected note without mentioning the change.
answer_b: Ignore the error if the patient received services somewhere else.
answer_c: Ask the referral agency to correct the RN note.
answer_d: Use the approved correction or addendum process so the original entry remains visible and the accurate agency is documented.
rationale: Corrections should preserve the original record, identify the accurate information, and follow organizational procedure.

question_text: Why is unchecked copy-forward documentation risky in RN case management?
answer_a: It may repeat stale information that no longer reflects referral status, patient risk, or follow-up needs.
answer_b: It is always prohibited in every documentation system.
answer_c: It eliminates the need to verify patient statements.
answer_d: It proves that the prior assessment is still current.
rationale: Copy-forward text must be verified and updated because stale information can hide changed risks or completed coordination steps.

question_text: A transportation vendor needs pickup details for a fictional patient appointment. Which communication best follows minimum necessary principles?
answer_a: Send the full discharge summary because it contains all possible context.
answer_b: Provide appointment time, pickup location, mobility needs, and contact instructions needed for transport.
answer_c: Share the patient's complete medication list so the vendor understands the condition.
answer_d: Avoid arranging transportation because confidentiality prevents any information sharing.
rationale: Minimum necessary communication shares the information needed for the specific coordination purpose and avoids unrelated clinical details.

question_text: Which documentation element is most important for showing that patient education was understood?
answer_a: A statement that education was done.
answer_b: A copied list of all discharge topics.
answer_c: The patient's teach-back response, barriers, and plan for reinforcement when needed.
answer_d: A note that the patient was told to read the packet.
rationale: Education documentation is stronger when it includes the topic, reason, learner response, understanding check, barriers, and follow-up plan.

question_text: A note says, "home health referral completed," but the receiving agency has not accepted the patient. What should the RN case manager document?
answer_a: Referral completed because an order exists.
answer_b: No further documentation until the first visit occurs.
answer_c: The patient declined all home services.
answer_d: Referral destination, transmission status, pending acceptance, follow-up plan, and any escalation if services are delayed.
rationale: Referral evidence should distinguish ordered, sent, accepted, scheduled, completed, and pending statuses so gaps are visible.

question_text: Which phrase best demonstrates attribution in a case management note?
answer_a: Patient's daughter stated the medication delivery did not arrive; RN contacted pharmacy at 1430.
rationale: Attribution identifies who supplied information and what the RN did with it, making the note more reliable and reviewable.

question_text: A patient reports medication confusion after discharge. Which RN case management response stays within scope and supports documentation?
answer_a: Tell the patient which medication to stop without provider clarification.
answer_b: Review the discharge list with the patient, identify the confusion, notify the provider pathway, document education, and track the response.
answer_c: Promise that the provider will approve a new medication schedule.
answer_d: Document only that the patient had questions.
rationale: The RN coordinates, educates, notifies, and follows up without creating independent provider orders or promising outcomes.

question_text: Which rewrite best repairs the subjective note "patient is difficult and refuses to listen"?
answer_a: Patient remains difficult despite repeated instruction.
answer_c: Patient stated she was overwhelmed by the medication changes; RN reviewed two priority instructions and patient correctly repeated when to call the provider.
answer_d: Patient will probably ignore instructions again.
rationale: Neutral documentation records the patient's statement, RN teaching, and response without judgmental labels.

question_text: During an audit, a reviewer cannot tell whether a provider callback occurred. Which documentation practice would most directly prevent this gap?
answer_a: Use a general phrase such as handled to save time.
answer_b: Wait to chart until the full case is closed.
answer_c: Document only the initial message because callbacks are part of normal workflow.
answer_d: Record the callback time, provider response, RN communication to the patient, and remaining follow-up.
rationale: Audit-ready records show the loop from concern to response and clarify what remains pending.

question_text: Why must learners avoid submitting real chart notes in the M04 Moodle assignment?
answer_a: Moodle records may retain submissions and instructor comments, so real patient or facility identifiers must not be included.
answer_b: Real chart notes are acceptable if names are removed but facility details remain.
answer_c: The assignment is optional and does not require documentation practice.
answer_d: Only provider notes are confidential; RN notes are not.
rationale: Course submissions can become retained education records, so learners must use only fictional facts and avoid patient or facility identifiers.

question_text: A learner passes the M04 quiz and submits the assignment while provider approval metadata is still unavailable. What should happen with certificate release?
answer_a: Release the certificate because the module quiz was passed.
answer_b: Keep certificate release blocked until provider approval metadata and all completion evidence gates are satisfied.
answer_c: Release a temporary certificate and replace it later.
answer_d: Treat M04 completion as enough to advertise approval status.
rationale: Module completion evidence does not authorize certificate release unless approval metadata and all course-level gates are satisfied.

question_text: Which documentation revision best closes the follow-up loop after a referral delay?
answer_c: Home health intake reported no staffing until Friday; RN notified provider office, reviewed safety precautions with patient using teach-back, and scheduled next-day follow-up call.
rationale: The revision identifies the barrier, communication, patient education, understanding check, and planned follow-up, making the record actionable.
